title: Arterial & Capillary Blood Gas Sampling Quiz

questions.text: Which three values are measured DIRECTLY from an arterial blood gas?
Which of the following is a contraindication to arterial puncture?
An ABG hazard that can result in loss of consciousness is:
Which artery is the preferred sampling site in a newborn during the first 24–48 hours of life?
Which artery is used as a scalp sampling site in infants?
Which is the preferred anticoagulant for ABG sampling?
Why are glass syringes no longer the standard for ABG collection?

answers.text: Glass is too expensive to manufacture
Plastic syringes are now the standard despite being more permeable to room air, which can skew PaO₂ if bubbles are present

questions.text: What is the purpose of placing an ABG sample on ice?

answers.text: To slow metabolic activity and preserve the sample

questions.text: What is the vessel of choice for arterial puncture in adults?
The Modified Allen test specifically assesses adequacy of which arterial circulation?
After releasing the ulnar artery during the Modified Allen test, the hand should flush within:
When performing a radial ABG, the wrist should be extended approximately:
The needle is inserted for a radial ABG at which angle and orientation?
How long should pressure be applied to the puncture site of a patient on anticoagulant therapy or with a bleeding disorder?
After obtaining an ABG sample, the syringe should be mixed by:

answers.text: Tapping firmly against a hard surface

questions.text: The most serious unique hazard of brachial artery puncture is:
When is femoral arterial puncture indicated?

answers.text: Routine ABG collection in stable patients
Emergencies — hypotensive patient with poor peripheral pulses
Patients with peripheral vascular disease of the leg

questions.text: Which is a major hazard specific to femoral artery puncture?

answers.text: Hidden bleeding due to the vessel's deep, inconspicuous location and almost nonexistent collateral circulation

questions.text: Which is an indication for an indwelling arterial line (A-line)?

answers.text: A single one-time arterial blood gas
Frequent arterial blood gas measurements and continuous blood pressure monitoring

questions.text: Which is a contraindication to placing an A-line?
Which of the following can cause a dampened arterial line waveform?

answers.text: Clot in the catheter, catheter tip against the vessel wall, or air bubbles/clot in the transducer

questions.text: If the transducer for an arterial line is positioned BELOW the level of the heart, the pressure reading will:
If the transducer for an arterial line is positioned ABOVE the level of the heart, the pressure reading will:
Blood is sampled from an A-line through which device?
A capillary blood gas can reliably estimate which of the following?
Why is capillary PO₂ of little clinical value?

answers.text: It overestimates oxygenation in newborns by exactly 10 mmHg
It is unreliable for estimating arterial oxygenation
It is impossible to obtain in pediatric patients
It is always normal regardless of the patient's condition

questions.text: A capillary blood gas should NOT be performed on a patient who is:

answers.text: A pediatric outpatient at a follow-up visit

questions.text: Which item from the capillary blood gas kit is used (with a magnet) to mix the sample inside the capillary tube?
What is the most common site for a capillary blood gas sample?

answers.text: The heel — lateral aspect of the plantar surface

questions.text: Squeezing the puncture site during a capillary blood gas results in:

answers.text: Faster sample collection with no impact on accuracy
Venous and lymphatic contamination of the sample
